Defense-in-depth against a silent mitmproxy-log format regression. Out of M9-S20 scope per explicit agent-C deferral.

Spec correction: amend 2026-04-21-...-design.md Part 3 'Liveness posture' section - deny-logger healthcheck probes GATEWAY_IP:10003 (bridge IP via $(hostname -i)), NOT 127.0.0.1:10003. Empirically verified in Phase 4: listener bound to bridge IP does not answer on loopback. Should ride into Phase 9 /review or a docs-only follow-up.

BUG M10-S3 Phase 8 discovery: UDP deny events carry post-DNAT destination (gateway_ip:10002) instead of pre-DNAT (user's real target). Root cause: IP_ORIGDSTADDR cmsg does not expose pre-DNAT destination for conntrack-DNAT'd UDP like TCP's SO_ORIGINAL_DST does. Fix path: add conntrack netlink lookup in deny-logger UDP handler. Blocks useful M10-S4 discovery workflow for UDP traffic (TCP is fine, works via SO_ORIGINAL_DST). Reproduction: Phase 8 test 2 in sandboxd/sandboxd/tests/m10_s3_end_to_end.rs which currently asserts post-DNAT tuple with explanatory comment.

M10-S3 I1 follow-up: pin Rust toolchain for deny-logger Dockerfile build. Options: (a) add rust-toolchain.toml at workspace root with channel='1.85.0', (b) pin Docker stage to specific rust:1.85-bookworm digest. Supply-chain hygiene + build reproducibility. Parallels the golang:1.22 pinning on CoreDNS builder.

M10-S3 I3 follow-up: surface flush_ticker panics in sandbox-deny-logger. Options: (a) include flush_ticker JoinHandle in main's tokio::select! so panic takes process down, (b) install process-level std::panic::set_hook that logs and aborts. Current design hides panics silently; liveness posture should propagate listener-task exits to process termination consistently.

M10-S3 I4 follow-up: extract transition-detection core of poll_and_emit_component_health into a pure helper in sandbox-core. Currently Phase 8 test 4 re-implements ~150 lines of monitor-loop logic inline with a 500ms poll override and forced last_healthy reset. Shared helper removes double-maintenance risk when production monitor evolves (extra component, transition predicate, restart-gated policy reapply). Non-blocking - current structure is a pragmatic trade-off with good explanatory comments.

Flaky: sandbox-deny-logger tcp::tests::tcp_respects_concurrency_cap fails under load (passed in isolation + on re-run). Likely a timing/ordering bug in the concurrency-cap test setup. Investigate and either stabilize (stricter barriers, longer sleeps, #[ignore] with explicit rationale, or redesign) or pin as known-flaky. Pre-existing from M10-S3 Phase 3.

daemon: propagate git clone failure from create --repo handler. Currently sandboxd/src/main.rs:805-820 logs a warning but returns CREATED even when the in-guest clone exited non-zero. Session ends up Running with empty /home/agent/workspace — test_m5_workspace::test_clone_repo observes this under full-suite load. Either fail the create call with SessionError::CloneFailed or leave session in a distinct Degraded state. Pre-existing since 2fd260c; surfaced during M10-S4 full E2E run.

M10-S6 docs sweep: spec text uses 'sandbox start' for session creation; implementation uses 'sandbox create'. Reconcile across spec + user-facing docs. Not a code change (flags land on create in M10-S5).

M10-S5 Phase 5a' Option B: live cargo fetch trace in a sandbox VM to empirically verify the cargo preset host list. Agent chose Option A (documented endpoints) to save the 30+ min VM boot cost. Fixture at sandbox-cli/tests/fixtures/cargo_fetch_trace.json currently matches documented endpoints; spec cites 'verified against documented endpoints'. Live verification can update the fixture if the observed host set differs. Non-blocking.

M10-S6: introduce durable fix for policy propagation cold-boot race. M10-S5 band-aided with POLICY_PROPAGATION_S=15s. Real fix: either sandbox policy status --wait CLI subcommand + daemon endpoint reporting 'all rules propagated to all enforcement layers', or per-batch 'propagated' confirmation logs in sandbox_core::dns_propagation. Cold-boot race currently fires on first E2E run after make gateway-image; runs 2+ pass. Not blocking but fragile.

M11: durable Envoy LDS/listener-ack in PropagationState. sandboxd/src/main.rs:2235 mark_propagated flips when listener YAML write returns, NOT when Envoy acks the new config. M10-S6 E2E surfaced: cargo curl Connection refused 53ms after --wait returns propagated=true, because cycle-2 listener rewrite may still be in flight. Fix: poll Envoy admin /config_dump (or /stats last_update_duration) for listener snapshot version before flipping propagated. Handoff 20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md hypothesis 1.

M11: stabilize test_github_repo_preset_scopes_to_one_repo. Pre-existing ~80%-pass-rate flake. DNS rotation (github.com → rotating IPs 140.82.112.x/121.x/113.x/114.x) vs nftables allow-set causes torvalds/linux fetch to land on IP not in the github-repo preset set → deny at L3 (deny-logger) instead of L7 (mitmproxy) where the assertion expects. Handoff 20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md failure 2 + prior 20260423-070500-* + 20260423-074500-*. Fix path: expand allow-set with suffix/CIDR matching for github.com pool, or add rule-level 'expand to common pool' semantics to the github-repo preset. Non-blocking for now.

Investigate real multi-host policy propagation race: curl TCP-RST within ~100ms post-propagated=true on presets with >1 host. Envoy listener_manager.lds.update_success stays at 0 (filesystem-watch no-op on content-identical rewrites). Candidates: (a) Envoy cluster STRICT_DNS endpoint resolution lag, (b) nftables set enforcement delay after add-element, (c) Envoy worker-thread filter-chain swap not atomic. See progress log discovery (post-fix rerun analysis) and .tasks/handoffs/20260423-m10-s6-e2e-regression.md. M11-S1 work — blocks M10-close.

M11+: Git LFS preset (github-lfs / lfs=true on github-repo and github-pr). Spec 'Known gaps' bullet. No partial leak in M10 tree; file when user hits it. Files: sandboxd/sandbox-cli/src/presets/builtin.rs (new entry).

M11+: daemon-level (non-session-scoped) lifecycle events endpoint. Spec 'Known gaps' item — currently events are per-session; operator-level events (daemon up/down, gateway-image-rebuild, CA-rotation) have no stream. Surface: GET /events on the daemon socket. Non-blocking.
Add Python-version sentinel to tests/e2e/.venv/.installed Makefile rule so venv rebuilds when interpreter version changes (e.g., embed python-X.Y in stamp filename).
Add clap/env to sandboxd's direct deps so 'cargo check -p sandboxd' works in isolation. Currently builds only via workspace feature unification through sandbox-deny-logger.

Integration test for #16: trigger a real PolicyDistributor::distribute failure end-to-end (syntactically-valid policy the gateway rejects) to exercise the fatal-failure path through the full create flow. Hermetic unit test currently pins the contract; this would catch regressions in the trigger path itself.
EVENTS_HOST_ROOT (/tmp/sandboxd-events) is the next candidate for XDG migration (same pattern as #20 listener path). Forward-pointing doc comment left in events/mod.rs for the next reader.

cargo fmt drift on m10-s8-polish-sweep: atomic_listener_writer.rs, main.rs, m10_s3_end_to_end.rs need fmt — accumulated across Groups 2-3 commits. Fold into the final session-close cleanup commit before opening a PR.

Investigate LDS in-place vs warm-restart-with-drain mismatch: design comment at sandbox-core/src/policy.rs:1158 claims filter-chain-only rewrites don't drain, but runtime shows listener_modified ticks + total_listeners_draining 0→1 even on valid-→valid rewrites. Hypothesis: Envoy in-place path applies only to changes within filter_chains: (named match list), not default_filter_chain: (catch-all). PolicyCompiler currently emits the latter for L1 passthrough. Either fix the compiler to use filter_chains: where in-place semantics matter, or correct the design comment. Discovered during Group 5 #22 investigation; full evidence in handoff 20260425-101213-m10-s8-group5-lds-test-hardening.md.

Gateway health check witnesses processes running, not an active listener. integration_gateway_lds_listener_and_atomic_rewrite shows gateway_status() == Healthy holds even when bootstrap listener is rejected by Envoy (total_listeners_active == 0). Health check should require at least one active listener after first successful LDS update. Discovered during Group 5 #22 investigation.

M10-S8 Group 5 follow-up: investigate in-place-vs-drain mismatch — design comment at sandbox-core/src/policy.rs:1158 says filter-chain-only rewrite is in-place; runtime stats show warm-restart-with-drain (listener_modified ticks, total_listeners_draining 0→1) for the test's emit shape (uses default_filter_chain:). Verify whether populated filter_chains: shape behaves the same. If yes, connection preservation across rule edits is not actually achieved.

M10-S8 Group 5 follow-up: gateway health check should witness an active listener, not just running processes. Current Healthy state passes when bootstrap listener is rejected by Envoy (total_listeners_active=0, lds.update_rejected=1). Production risk: a policy-compiled listener rejected at runtime would still report Healthy in GatewayManager.

Symmetry follow-up to #34: boot_cmd block in create_session (sandboxd/sandboxd/src/main.rs ~line 924-980) still has the warn-and-continue pattern for guest exec failures. If --boot-cmd fails, session ends up Running anyway. Consider extending fail_explicit_repo_clone-style fatal-failure path to boot_cmd for symmetry. Out of scope for #34 (no failing E2E).
M10-S5: pytest cleanup deletes session events on remove — set SANDBOX_KEEP_SESSION_EVENTS or test-side preservation flag for E2E debugging (mitmproxy.jsonl + deny-logger.jsonl)

M11: close the DNS-rotation propagation race architecturally. CoreDNS resolves new IP and answers VM in <1ms, but sandboxd dns_propagation_loop polls resolved.json every 2s — VM's TCP arrives at gateway nft set before the IP is admitted, deny-logger RSTs. Phase 3's CARGO_FASTLY_CIDR_POOL (9d1dca7) and #39 GITHUB_INTERACTIVE_CIDR_POOL (874a9bb) work around this for known CDN supernets but don't generalize. Two viable architectural fixes: (C) inline nft add element from sandboxpolicy CoreDNS plugin's WriteMsg interceptor BEFORE returning DNS answer to VM (race-free, cross-language coordination — Go plugin writes nft state managed by Rust daemon, risk of divergence with rebuild-from-cache model); (D) synchronous gating where CoreDNS plugin holds DNS response until sandboxd acks nft update (file/fifo barrier, adds DNS latency but architecturally clean). Both require redesigning the propagation contract; out of scope for polish session. Investigation handoff in M10-S8 progress log.

Lima SSH essential-requirement startup timeout (~22s default) caused a 1-in-3 E2E flake during M10-S8 Group 7 verification on 8GB host. If pattern recurs across delegations, tune sandbox-core::lima ssh.essentialRequirement retry budget upward (~45s) so VM has headroom under post-teardown IO pressure. Single occurrence so far — file only after second sighting. Evidence: .tasks/handoffs/20260425-135200-m10-s8-group7-e2e-lima-ssh-flake.md run 2.

nft set-injection duplicate-element noise: when two allowed hosts share an IP (e.g. archive.ubuntu.com + security.ubuntu.com both resolving to 91.189.91.81), nft batch logs 'File exists' for the second add even though policy still propagates correctly. Cleanup options: (a) dedupe IPs before nft batch insertion, (b) use 'add element ... { ... }' with idempotent semantics. Low-priority log-noise item, orthogonal to correctness. Discovered during M10-S8 Group 10 live-trace VM run.

Promote live-cargo-trace methodology to an opt-in slow E2E test for periodic toolchain-bump re-verification. Current state: one-off harness was deleted post-todo-#36 verification; test_cargo_preset_allows_cargo_fetch remains as lighter ongoing coverage. Methodology: custom-policy VM + apt-install cargo + capture mitmproxy events filtered by RFC3339-ms timestamp marker between phases. Mark with pytest.mark.slow/manual gate so make test-e2e doesn't run it by default. Out of M10-S8 scope; useful when cargo upstream changes its sparse-protocol or registry topology.

Drift-detection unit test cargo_preset_matches_frozen_trace currently only compares hosts[].host strings against preset domains — does not catch drift in methods/purpose/notes fields. If a future cargo version introduces a new HTTP method or changes a host's role, the test stays green even though the fixture has rotted. Consider extending the assertion to compare methods sets too, or accept the gap as documentation-only and add a comment. Low-priority. Surfaced during M10-S8 Group 10 review.

remember.text: KVM access: /dev/kvm needs chmod 666 or newgrp kvm in this VM. Group membership doesn't take effect without re-login. Fix with: sudo chmod 666 /dev/kvm
Lima does NOT support kernel AF_VSOCK. Using TCP-over-SSH-tunnel instead. Guest agent listens on TCP port inside VM, host connects via Lima SSH port forward. Protocol unchanged (framed JSON).
VM networking uses two NICs: eth0=SLIRP (Lima SSH/management), eth1=TAP on Docker bridge (data, hot-added via QMP at ~/.lima/{vm}/qmp.sock). Guest agent configures eth1 static IP.

Privilege model: sandboxd runs as regular user (docker+kvm groups). nftables via docker exec with CAP_NET_ADMIN in container. TAP devices via qemu-bridge-helper (setuid, ships with QEMU). No sudo/sudoers/root anywhere. Host-side bridge/TAP/veth code in network.rs to be fully removed.
Run failing E2E tests individually during debugging. Only run full suite for final validation.
Host VM has 8 CPUs and 8GB RAM. Session VMs: 2 CPUs / 2 GB. Base image build VM: 4 CPUs / 4 GB (needs more resources for cloud-init + apt). No timeout may exceed 600s (10 min).

Slow cloud-init root cause: flaky HTTP connections to Ubuntu mirrors (apt default 120s timeout per stalled connection). Fix: HTTPS sources + /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/99sandbox with 5s timeout, 5 retries, ForceIPv4. Base image build now ~82s.

Subagent delegation gotcha: subagents may ignore 'do not touch session-tracking' instructions and call the progress CLI. Orchestrator must verify session state after each return. Also: LSP diagnostics can be stale; ground-truth verification is cargo build from sandboxd/ workspace root.

Gateway container image must rebuild whenever networking/ changes. Makefile test-e2e depends on stamp-driven gateway-image target. Host sandboxd ↔ gateway addon version drift is silent when the addon falls back to allow-all on unknown shape — pin both sides with unit tests (see policy.rs mitmproxy_config_matches_e2e_failing_policies and test_policy_addon.py::TestLegacyPreM9S10ShapeIsRejected).

Session plan moved to docs/internal/session-plan.md during M9-S16. progress.json plan field still points at docs/session-plan.md — a future progress CLI update should fix it or ignore when referring to plan.
